next_steps: Perform Bartonella henselae serology (IgG and IgM) and PCR testing
Order DHR (dihydrorhodamine) flow cytometry to screen for Chronic Granulomatous Disease
Perform bone or liver biopsy for histopathology, culture, and PCR (specifically for Bartonella and mycobacteria)
Obtain complete blood count, erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR), and C-reactive protein (CRP)
Consult Pediatric Infectious Disease and Immunology services
Hold immunosuppressive therapy pending results of diagnostic workup
Perform an ophthalmologic examination to look for Bartonella retinitis
